evidence_text: In my case: the Claude Code desktop app makes having a bunch of parallel sessions easy, at least compared to when I had just a bunch of terminal windows open can also couple that with Remote Control Previously I still had the issue of it occasionally stopping let's say after Stage 2/7 is done in some plan and asking me to continue, though I was asleep.
human_notes: Claude Code desktop app makes parallel sessions easier than terminal windows, though it sometimes stops for continuation.

evidence_text: You can get really far with the 20x Claude Code and Codex plans. They are many orders of magnitude cheaper than api calls.
human_notes: Strong positive value claim that Claude Code plans go far and are orders of magnitude cheaper than API calls.

evidence_text: Don't use Quillbot; not sure why, but their model is reluctant to classify anything as AI generated. I ran into this when proof-reading a students Phd - ChatGPT, Gemini, CLaude (and others) all agreed it was AI generated, but Quillbot said it wasn't.
human_notes: Primary claim is that Quillbot performed poorly at AI-generated-text classification compared with ChatGPT/Gemini/Claude.

evidence_text: Don't use Quillbot; not sure why, but their model is reluctant to classify anything as AI generated. I ran into this when proof-reading a students Phd - ChatGPT, Gemini, CLaude (and others) all agreed it was AI generated, but Quillbot said it wasn't.
human_notes: Primary claim is that Quillbot failed to classify AI-generated text while ChatGPT/Gemini/Claude agreed.

evidence_text: I think teams need to be able to write nested workflows that transition between code-led and agent-led, with either supporting human-in-the-loop checkpoints. Been iterating on what this should look like at our startup ( ). Model labs are also improving capabilities here, such as Codex's `/goal` and Claude Code's dynamic workflows[1] The points about API usage cost still stand, but model intelligence is getting cheaper every month!
human_notes: Mentions Claude Code dynamic workflows as an improving capability for nested agent/code workflows.

evidence_text: I had a similar experience where I have a DB user who only has access to some parts of the DB and I use Claude Code to answer queries. On one occasion, unable to answer something, it used kubectl to try to get a secret out of a prod cluster. I was watching at the time, since that was a time I used to do that, and so I just hit Esc and interrupted it[0] but ever since then I've had little scripts in my repos that launch kubectl-backed Claude Code instances for this sort of thing.
human_notes: Claude Code tried to use kubectl to retrieve a production secret when unable to answer a query.

evidence_text: I had a similar experience where I have a DB user who only has access to some parts of the DB and I use Claude Code to answer queries. On one occasion, unable to answer something, it used kubectl to try to get a secret out of a prod cluster.
human_notes: Claude Code tried to use kubectl to extract a production secret after failing to answer a DB query.
